item_description: de Buyer - Sauté Pan with cast iron handle - 4 Sizes Ø 16 - 28 cm - Ideal for quickly browning food, reduction cooking...
overview:
de Buyer - Sauté Pan with cast iron handle - Inocuivre First Classe - Rounded sauté-pan perfect for reduction cooking and especially for reducing sauces: a small base for fast heating and a wide top diameter for perfect moisture evaporation. It enables an elegant display for the table.
Inocuivre First Classe cookware composed of 90% copper and 10% AISI 304 stainless steel:


	90% copper on the outside: excellent thermal conductivity; exceptional cooking results.
	10% stainless steel on the inside: perfectly food grade; eliminates the need to re-tin; easy care. The low proportion of stainless steel does not alter the properties of copper.


Riveted solid cast iron handle. High-end mirror polishing. Prestigious and noble range that can be brought into the dining room, ideal for serving and flambéing.
Care of copper: use a polishing paste.
Care of stainless steel: wash by hand.


	Ø 24 cm  (Ø bottom = 21 cm)  H= 7 cm L (with Handle) = 49,5 cm - V= 3,1 L
	2 mm thick copper
	Handle: cast iron
	For all cooktops EXCEPT induction.


Beware of scams: some utensils presented as stainless steel copper have a thin veneer of copper and none of its culinary qualities. The copper-stainless steel INOCUIVRE DE BUYER is an assurance of quality cooking with a material composed of 90% copper and 10% stainless steel, a single-piece skirt and bottom. This material diffuses heat evenly and quickly: not only on the bottom, but also on the sides; a heating circuit is created and the food is cooked evenly. Ideal for delicate cooking, for sauces and generally for reduction cooking. Riveted cast iron long handle and side handles.

Copper:


	Best heat conductor: uniform distribution and ideal transmission of heat.
	Allows greater control of the cooking environment.
	Preserves the natural colour of fruit: jams do not brown.
	Large flat bottom: very stable pan even on a gas burner.
	Rounded angle formed by the skirt and flat bottom: allows you to work all the mixture; easy maintenance.
	cast iron handles: easy handling; safe use.
	Open rolled edge: hygienic; no protruding edge.
	Care: soak. Easy cleaning with a special copper polish paste.
	Dishwasher-safe but without alkaline detergent cleaning.
	Suitable for all cooktops EXCEPT induction.


Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

item_description: de Buyer - Copper Saucepan - Inocuivre - This saucepan is a truly professional French culinary tool.
overview:
The de Buyer Inocuivre saucepan combines a technical side with aesthetic qualities and provides a generous size with a rounded interior. Copper is the material that sets the standard for heat diffusion: it provides perfect and controlled heat diffusion across the entire pan for even cooking on any cook top EXCEPT induction. This saucepan is a truly professional French culinary tool. The long, elegant, ergonomic cast stainless steel handle, firmly fastened with rivets, guarantees a natural and comfortable grip.


Ø 20 cm  (Ø bottom = 16 cm)  H= 11,5 cm - V= 3,5 Liter	
90% COPPER- 10% STAINLESS STEEL
	2-mm thick copper stainless steel - Gloss polished
	For all cooktops EXCEPT induction.
	Riveted ergonomic handles made of cast st/steel.
	Excellent conductor of heat
	Uniform cooking through both bottom and sides
	Ideal for reducing and simmering


PRIMA MATERA - For cooking and for presentation on table. Copper is an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. This material ensures quick and homogeneous cooking in the whole pan: not only in the bottom but also on the side to obtain a perfect heat distribution which makes copper pans ideal for delicate preparations, such as sauces.
 



Outer COPPER - the colour changes on a heat sources. Use a special copper cleaner to give brilliance back to a copper utensil. Never use a metal sponge. The stainless steel inner wall allows for easy care. There is no need for replating as for the traditional tin-plated copper.

Authenticity

Care

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Utensils 2 mm thick are intended for professional use. Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

Handles in cast steel, curved, fixed by strong rivets. Copper-stainless steel: this is a bimetal laminate.

The outer sides are made of very thick copper and allow excellent heat conduction. The inner side, made of stainless steel, allow for easy care of the pans and there is no need for replating. Polished outer finish. Mountings in cast steel, stainless steel, or bronze, fixed by strong rivets.

The MINERAL B ELEMENT pan will be part of your kitchenware for decades, as long as you follow the specific instructions to use and clean the iron utensils.

The ‘B BOIS’ line is the result of the meeting of 2 worlds, 2 brands and offers a whole range of new products, from frying pan to spice mills, including essential utensils. The beech wood used is a PEFC certified wood coming from sustainably managed forests. It is natural and protected with beeswax. The ‘B BOIS’ products are exclusively sold through physical retailers.

The essential MINERAL B ELEMENT steel range comes with a beech wood handle protected by beeswax, as for the pan body. Always essential for high temperature cooking, for sealing, frying and browning.

The MINERAL B ELEMENT frying pan has a long iron handle firmly fastened with rivets. It features a true Lyonnaise shape: the side is high, flared and rounded, without any sharp angles, which means it lets the food slide easily from the pan to the dish or plate. The fry pan becomes naturally non-stick the more it is used thanks to the seasoning process. The extra-strong iron of this pan is suitable for intense sources of heat, including induction. The MINERAL B ELEMENT pan will be part of your kitchenware for decades, as long as you follow the specific instructions to use and clean the iron utensils.

Beware of scams: some utensils presented as stainless steel copper have a thin veneer of copper and none of its culinary qualities. The copper-stainless steel INOCUIVRE DE BUYER is an assurance of quality cooking with a material composed of 90% copper and 10% stainless steel, a single-piece skirt and bottom. This material diffuses heat evenly and quickly: not only on the bottom, but also on the sides; a heating circuit is created and the food is cooked evenly. Ideal for delicate cooking, for sauces and generally for reduction cooking. Riveted brass long handle and side handles.

Copper:


	Best heat conductor: uniform distribution and ideal transmission of heat.
	Allows greater control of the cooking environment.
	Preserves the natural colour of fruit: jams do not brown.
	Large flat bottom: very stable pan even on a gas burner.
	Rounded angle formed by the skirt and flat bottom: allows you to work all the mixture; easy maintenance.
	Brass handles: easy handling; safe use.
	Open rolled edge: hygienic; no protruding edge.
	Care: soak. Easy cleaning with a special copper polish paste.
	Dishwasher-safe but without alkaline detergent cleaning.
	Suitable for all cooktops EXCEPT induction.


Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

WORLDWIDE UNIQUENESS COPPER COOKWARE NOW INDUCTION READY


	with Lid
	90% COPPER- 10% STAINLESS STEEL
	2-mm thick copper stainless steel - Gloss polished
	Special innovative ferro-magnetic bottom
	for all hotplates including INDUCTION
	Riveted ergonomic handles made of cast st/steel.
	Copper Stewpan with magnetic bottom INDUCTION and st/steel lid

The manufacturer does not guarantee the flatness of the floor, if the diameter of the heat source deviates more than 1,5cm in relation to the bottom of the pan. There are no restrictions for the use of iron pans on full surface induction and gas stoves.






Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Utensils 2 mm thick are intended for professional use. Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

Handles in cast steel, curved, fixed by strong rivets. Copper-stainless steel: this is a bimetal laminate.

The outer sides are made of very thick copper and allow excellent heat conduction. The inner side, made of stainless steel, allow for easy care of the pans and there is no need for replating. Polished outer finish. Mountings in cast steel, stainless steel, or bronze, fixed by strong rivets.

It can be used to prepare large amounts of food and its high sides limit splashes when cooking meat.  Can be used with the removable LOQY handle or with the pair of LOQY beech wood handles.  The MINERAL B  LOQI "Lyonnaise-shape" frying pan: the side is high, flared and rounded, without any sharp angles, enabling the food to slide easily from the pan to the dish or plate. The fry pan becomes naturally non-stick the more it is used thanks to the seasoning process. The extra-strong iron of this pan is suitable for intense sources of heat, including induction. The MINERAL B pan will be part of your kitchenware for decades, as long as you follow the specific instructions to use and clean the iron utensils. The blacker it gets, the less it sticks.

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Utensils 2 mm thick are intended for professional use. Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

Handles in cast steel, curved, fixed by strong rivets. Copper-stainless steel: this is a bimetal laminate.

The outer sides are made of very thick copper and allow excellent heat conduction. The inner side, made of stainless steel, allow for easy care of the pans and there is no need for replating. Polished outer finish. Mountings in cast steel, stainless steel, or bronze, fixed by strong rivets.

overview:
Dough Sheeter: Perfect Doughs – Effortlessly by Hand

With the dough sheeter from de Buyer, you bring professional precision into your kitchen. Whether for delicate patisserie, handmade pasta, or crispy pizza bases – this machine, developed in France, combines thoughtful design, outstanding build quality, and exceptional functionality. Ideal for anyone who values perfect results and effortless operation.

The professional dough sheeter was developed in France and meets the highest standards for both professional kitchens and home use. Perfect for evenly rolling out a wide variety of doughs such as puff pastry, pizza dough, sugar paste, marzipan, or pasta.

Thanks to the innovative hand crank, the dough can be rolled efficiently in both directions – without deformation or rebound. The extra-wide roller (34 cm) and the continuously adjustable thickness setting from 0.3 to 15 mm provide maximum flexibility, whether you need ultra-thin sheets or thicker dough.

Advantages of the Dough Sheeter:


	Large rolling surface: 34 cm wide roller – ideal for larger quantities of dough
	Precise thickness adjustment: continuously adjustable from 0.3 to 15 mm
	No reverse rolling: roll in both directions without deforming the dough
	Stable & robust: made from glass-fiber-reinforced polyamide with stainless steel components
	Easy to clean: smooth surfaces, can be disassembled for easy cleaning (not dishwasher safe)
	Versatile use: for puff pastry, pizza dough, sugar paste, marzipan, pasta dough, and more

overview:
AIRMAT Perforated Baking Mat – Special for Choux Pastry

The AIRMAT silicone baking mat is perfect for choux pastry lovers. Éclairs, religieuses, and Paris-Brest will be flawlessly executed thanks to the guides printed on both the front and back of this de Buyer baking mat. With professional quality, this choux pastry baking mat is suitable for household ovens with dimensions of 40x30 cm.

Versatile, you can freeze your preparations directly on this Silicone baking mesh AIRMAT and then bake them straight from the freezer. This professional silicone baking mat withstands temperatures ranging from -76°C to +260°C.

The guides on this special choux pastry mesh will allow you to easily pipe éclairs of 12 cm or choux pastries of various diameters (3, 6, or 8 cm). This ensures uniform preparations arranged to guarantee optimal baking. The placement of the guides also allows you to pipe perfectly even Paris-Brest pastries. For even more precision while piping, use the de Buyer Tube.

When combined with a microperforated baking tray, you will achieve perfect baking for your choux pastry, as well as viennoiseries or tarts. Although the AIRMAT silicone baking mesh is designed for choux pastry, it is also ideal for baking dry doughs such as shortcrust, puff, or sable dough. Thanks to its perforated design, heat distribution is even, allowing you to achieve delightfully crispy tart bases.

The goat's cheese bavarois and tomato jelly
by Philippe Laruelle

For a log mould 30 cm Réf : 3205.04

Ingredients

Tomato jelly


	75 g of tomato juice
	2 gr of gelatine
	Celery salt (don't leave it out, but amount is based on personal preference)


Bavarois


	100 g of fresh goat's cheese
	50 g of Single Cream
	50 g of chicken broth
	2 g of gelatine
	25 g of chopped Kalamata olives
	25 g of chopped sundried tomatoes



Instructions

Boil the tomato juice and add the gelatine that you have previously rehydrated in cold water. Mix well and pour into the bottom of the mould.
Leave to set in the fridge.


	Boil the chicken stock (white stock) and the cream.
	Melt the gelatine that has previously been rehydrated in cold water.
	Pour in 3 batches over the fresh goat's cheese, and whisk to obtain a smooth mixture.
	Add the olives and tomatoes.


Mix and pour over the tomato jelly.
Leave to set in the fridge for at least 3 hours.

Enjoy with rocket salad for example.

overview:
de Buyer - Sauté-Pan with 2 cast Iron handles and lid - Sauté-pan with 2 handles and lid, ideal for quickly browning food, perfect for service, for reduction cooking, reheating, stewing, simmering... It enables an elegant display for the table.
Inocuivre First Classe cookware composed of 90% copper and 10% AISI 304 stainless steel:


	90% copper on the outside: excellent thermal conductivity; exceptional cooking results.
	10% stainless steel on the inside: perfectly food grade; eliminates the need to re-tin; easy care. The low proportion of stainless steel does not alter the properties of copper.


Riveted solid cast Iron handles. High-end mirror polishing. Prestigious and noble range that can be brought into the dining room, ideal for serving and flambéing.
Care of copper: use a polishing paste.
Care of stainless steel: wash by hand.


	Ø 20 cm  (Ø Bottom = 18 cm)  H= 6 cm - L (with Handle) 28 cm - V= 1,5 L
	2 mm thick copper
	Handle: cast Iron
	For all cooktops EXCEPT induction.


Beware of scams: some utensils presented as stainless steel copper have a thin veneer of copper and none of its culinary qualities. The copper-stainless steel INOCUIVRE DE BUYER is an assurance of quality cooking with a material composed of 90% copper and 10% stainless steel, a single-piece skirt and bottom. This material diffuses heat evenly and quickly: not only on the bottom, but also on the sides; a heating circuit is created and the food is cooked evenly. Ideal for delicate cooking, for sauces and generally for reduction cooking. Riveted cast Iron long handle and side handles.

Copper:


	Best heat conductor: uniform distribution and ideal transmission of heat.
	Allows greater control of the cooking environment.
	Preserves the natural colour of fruit: jams do not brown.
	Large flat bottom: very stable pan even on a gas burner.
	Rounded angle formed by the skirt and flat bottom: allows you to work all the mixture; easy maintenance.
	cast Iron handles: easy handling; safe use.
	Open rolled edge: hygienic; no protruding edge.
	Care: soak. Easy cleaning with a special copper polish paste.
	Dishwasher-safe but without alkaline detergent cleaning.
	Suitable for all cooktops EXCEPT induction.


Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

overview:

	Oven-safe.
	Goes in the freezer, deep-freezer, blast chiller, refrigerator. Oven-safe.
	Not suitable for the microwave oven.
	Care: dishwasher-safe.
	Several shapes and sizes available








 

The Mango Passion Fruit Vanilla Yule Log
by Jonathan Mougel

For a log mould 30 cm ref 3206.30 + mini log mould ref 3206.04

Ingredients

Almond genoise


	2 whole eggs
	65 g of sugar
	65 g of T55 flour
	25 g of ground almonds


Crisp


	68 g of almond praline
	10 g of white chocolate
	1/4 vanilla pod
	40 g of crumbled gavotte biscuits
	1 g of Fleur de sel
	20 g Toasted chopped almonds


Mango-passion fruit filling


	75 g of Mango Pulp
	200 g of Pineapple brunoise
	 2 g of Ginger
	26 g of Brown sugar
	Lime zest of one lime
	1,2 g of Agar-agar
	1.5 g of NH Pectin (or 1.5 g of gelatin rehydrated in cold water)
	2 g of gelatin


Vanilla Mousse


	170 g of milk
	2 g of vanilla
	50 g of egg yolks
	30 g of sugar
	3 g of gelatin
	100 g of white chocolate
	240 g of whipped cream


Exotic glaze


	250 g of exotic fruit pulp
	50 gof lemon pulp
	75 g of glucose
	100 g of sugar
	5 g of NH pectin (or 4 g of gelatin rehydrated in cold water)
	5 g of gelatin
	100 g of neutral mirror glaze



Instructions

Almond genoise (for 1 sheet of 40x30cm) (1 base 7x29.5cm) :


	Whisk the eggs and sugar in a double boiler until the mixture is frothy. Do not exceed 50°C
	Add  the sifted flour. Then add the almonds gently.
	Spread on a 40x30 sheet.
	Bake for 8 minutes at 175°C.
	Cut a base of 7x29.5cm.


Crisp (138 g per log) :

Melt the white chocolate and add the vanilla.


	Then add the praline and mix.
	Mix all the ingredients together.
	Spread over the base.
	Set aside.


Mango-passion fruit filling :


	Cook all the ingredients for 2 min.
	Add the gelatin with the lime zest at the end.
	Pour into a mini log mould and freeze the filling.


Vanilla mousse (590 g per mould) :


	Heat the milk, vanilla, egg yolks and sugar.
	Cook everything at 85°C.
	Add the melted gelatin and pour it all into the chocolate.
	Mix. Add the whipped cream when it reaches 23°C.


Exotic glaze :


	To be prepared the day before.
	Heat the pulps to 40°C, add the pectin-sugar mixture.
	Bring to the boil.
	Add the previously hydrated gelatin, then the mirror glaze..
	Mix.
	The next day, heat to 30°C and mix.
	Glaze the log.


Assembly and finishing :


	Place an acetate sheet in the bottom of the mould.
	Pour in the vanilla mousse and immerse the exotic filling..
	Seal with the base/crisp.
	Freeze for at least 6 hours.
	Unmould and glaze with the exotic glaze.
	Decorate.

Riveted brass long handle and side handles.Copper:	Best heat conductor: uniform distribution and ideal transmission of heat.	Allows greater control of the cooking environment.	Preserves the natural colour of fruit: jams do not brown.	Large flat bottom: very stable pan even on a gas burner.	Rounded angle formed by the skirt and flat bottom: allows you to work all the mixture; easy maintenance.	Brass handles: easy handling; safe use.	Open rolled edge: hygienic; no protruding edge.	Care: soak. Easy cleaning with a special copper polish paste.	Dishwasher-safe but without alkaline detergent cleaning.	Suitable for all cooktops EXCEPT induction.AuthenticityThe nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.CareTo clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.Technical featuresCopper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

The utensils in the de Buyer PRIMA MATERA range are 90% copper and 10% stainless steel on the inside. Copper is the best heat conductor and ensures a perfect heating circuit throughout the entire container. It provides exceptional cooking performance on any cook top, including induction, through the special iron-magnetic stainless steel INDUCTION bottom.
Elegant and fluid, the ergonomic long handles and side handles of the range are made with cast stainless steel.

WORLDWIDE UNIQUENESS COPPER COOKWARE NOW INDUCTION READY


	90% COPPER- 10% STAINLESS STEEL
	2-mm thick copper stainless steel - Gloss polished
	Special innovative ferro-magnetic bottom
	for all hotplates including INDUCTION
	Riveted ergonomic handles made of cast st/steel.
	Copper Stewpan with magnetic bottom INDUCTION and st/steel lid
	Excellent conductor of heat
	Uniform cooking through both bottom and sides
	Ideal for reducing and simmering





Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Utensils 2 mm thick are intended for professional use. Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

Handles in cast steel, curved, fixed by strong rivets. Copper-stainless steel: this is a bimetal laminate.

The outer sides are made of very thick copper and allow excellent heat conduction. The inner side, made of stainless steel, allow for easy care of the pans and there is no need for replating. Polished outer finish. Mountings in cast steel, stainless steel, or bronze, fixed by strong rivets.

item_description: de Buyer - Sauté Pan with cast iron handle - Prima Matera - Ideal for quickly browning food, reduction cooking...
overview:
de Buyer - Sauté Pan with cast iron handle - Rounded sauté-pan perfect for reduction cooking and especially for reducing sauces: a small base for fast heating and a wide top diameter for perfect moisture evaporation. It enables an elegant display for the table.


	Ø 24 cm  (Ø bottom = 21,5 cm)  H= 7 cm L (with Handle) = 46 cm - V= 3 L
	90% copper on the outside: excellent thermal conductivity; exceptional cooking results.
	10% stainless steel on the inside: perfectly food grade; eliminates the need to re-tin; easy care. The low proportion of stainless steel does not alter the properties of copper.


Riveted solid cast iron handle. High-end mirror polishing. Prestigious and noble range that can be brought into the dining room, ideal for serving and flambéing.
Care of copper: use a polishing paste.
Care of stainless steel: wash by hand.


	2 mm thick copper
	90% COPPER- 10% STAINLESS STEEL
	2-mm thick copper stainless steel - Gloss polished
	Special innovative ferro-magnetic bottom
	for all hotplates including INDUCTION
	cast iron handles
	Copper Stewpan with magnetic bottom INDUCTION and st/steel lid
	Excellent conductor of heat
	Uniform cooking through both bottom and sides
	Ideal for reducing and simmering


Beware of scams: some utensils presented as stainless steel copper have a thin veneer of copper and none of its culinary qualities. The copper-stainless steel INOCUIVRE DE BUYER is an assurance of quality cooking with a material composed of 90% copper and 10% stainless steel, a single-piece skirt and bottom. This material diffuses heat evenly and quickly: not only on the bottom, but also on the sides; a heating circuit is created and the food is cooked evenly. Ideal for delicate cooking, for sauces and generally for reduction cooking. Riveted cast iron long handle and side handles.

Copper:




	Best heat conductor: uniform distribution and ideal transmission of heat.
	Allows greater control of the cooking environment.
	Preserves the natural colour of fruit: jams do not brown.
	Large flat bottom: very stable pan even on a gas burner.
	Rounded angle formed by the skirt and flat bottom: allows you to work all the mixture; easy maintenance.
	cast iron handles: easy handling; safe use.
	Open rolled edge: hygienic; no protruding edge.
	Care: soak. Easy cleaning with a special copper polish paste.
	Dishwasher-safe but without alkaline detergent cleaning.


Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

item_description: de Buyer - B Bois - Mineral B Element Triple-Blini Pan - cooking of 3 blinis, 3 fritters or 3 pancakes of 10 cm diameter.
overview:
Utensil which enables the simultaneous cooking of 3 blinis, 3 fritters or 3 pancakes of 10cm diameter.

The ‘B BOIS’ line is the result of the meeting of 2 worlds, 2 brands and offers a whole range of new products, from frying pan to spice mills, including essential utensils. The beech wood used is a PEFC certified wood coming from sustainably managed forests. It is natural and protected with beeswax. The ‘B BOIS’ products are exclusively sold through physical retailers.

The essential MINERAL B ELEMENT steel range comes with a beech wood handle protected by beeswax, as for the pan body. Always essential for high temperature cooking, for sealing, frying and browning.

The MINERAL B ELEMENT frying pan has a long iron handle firmly fastened with rivets. It features a true Lyonnaise shape: the side is high, flared and rounded, without any sharp angles, which means it lets the food slide easily from the pan to the dish or plate. The fry pan becomes naturally non-stick the more it is used thanks to the seasoning process. The extra-strong iron of this pan is suitable for intense sources of heat, including induction. The MINERAL B ELEMENT pan will be part of your kitchenware for decades, as long as you follow the specific instructions to use and clean the iron utensils.

Iron and cast iron pans are not suitable for scrambled eggs. Please only prepare setting eggs and omelets in a well-baked pan.

ORGANIC BEE WAX FINISH


	ø 27 cm - 3 blinis ø 10 cm
	Bees produce by-products that are well known in nature. de Buyer discovered that bee wax is an excellent option to protect their pans against oxidation.
	Bee wax helps in the seasoning process.
	Bee wax improves the nonstick quality.


IRON=100 % NATURAL MINERAL MATERIAL


	Guaranteed without PTFE and PFOA. No chemicals added.
	Environment safe production.
	Ecological, recyclable, and good for the environment.
                Only the universal lid for pans 3460 fits on this pan.
                Can be used on all types of cooker - incl. induction
                All information about the pans from de Buyer you can find on data sheets tab.


COOKING QUALITY


	High temperature: Perfect for SEALING, BROWNING and GRILLING
	Improved flavour.


TRADITIONAL


	Authentic design: «Lyonnaise» shape. Curved handle «à la Française». Symbol of French Gastronomy.


SEASONING


	The more the pan is used, the better the performance.
	The darker it becomes the better it is for natural nonstick properties.


FOR ALL HOTPLATES INCLUDING INDUCTION.

The manufacturer does not guarantee the flatness of the floor, if the diameter of the heat source deviates more than 1,5cm in relation to the bottom of the pan. There are no restrictions for the use of iron pans on full surface induction and gas stoves.

item_description: de Buyer - Roasting Pan 35 x 25 cm - Inocuivre - rectangular roasting tray, perfect for oven cooking meat, roasts, fish, vegetables..
overview:
de Buyer - Roasting Pan 35 x 25 cm - Inocuivre - Multilayer (copper/aluminium/St-steel) rectangular roasting tray, perfect for oven cooking meat, roasts, fish, potatoes, vegetables...
Flared edges and rounded corners for easy cleaning.
Equipped with 2 riveted cast stainless steel handles : robust.
Laser marking. This model is also perfect for presentation at the table. The stainless steel interior is easy to clean.


	Inside dimensions : L 35 x W 25 x H 8 cm
	Overall dimensions : 41 x 27,5 x 12,35 cm
	Multilayer - Copper/Aluminum/Steel
	2-mm thick copper stainless steel - Gloss polished
	for all hotplates - except induction
	Riveted ergonomic handles made of cast st/steel.
	Excellent conductor of heat
	Uniform cooking through both bottom and sides
	Ideal for reducing and simmering


Inocuivre - For cooking and for presentation on table. Copper is an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. This material ensures quick and homogeneous cooking in the whole pan: not only in the bottom but also on the side to obtain a perfect heat distribution which makes copper pans ideal for delicate preparations, such as sauces.



Outer COPPER - the colour changes on a heat sources. Use a special copper cleaner to give brilliance back to a copper utensil. Never use a metal sponge. The stainless steel inner wall allows for easy care. There is no need for replating as for the traditional tin-plated copper.

Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Utensils 2 mm thick are intended for professional use. Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

Handles in cast steel, curved, fixed by strong rivets. Copper-stainless steel: this is a bimetal laminate.

The outer sides are made of very thick copper and allow excellent heat conduction. The inner side, made of stainless steel, allow for easy care of the pans and there is no need for replating. Polished outer finish. Mountings in cast steel, stainless steel, or bronze, fixed by strong rivets.

item_description: de Buyer - Small saucepan with brass handles - Inocuivre - Small saucepan perfect for an elegant serving of sauces on the table.
overview:
Small saucepan perfect for an elegant serving of sauces on the table.
Inocuivre VIP cookware composed of 90% copper and 10% AISI 304 stainless steel:


	Ø 9 cm  (Ø Boden = 8,5 cm)  H= 4,5 cm   V= 0,3 L
	90% copper on the outside: excellent thermal conductivity; exceptional cooking results.
	10% stainless steel on the inside: perfectly food grade; eliminates the need to re-tin; easy care. The low proportion of stainless steel does not alter the properties of copper.


Riveted solid brass handles. High-end mirror polishing. Prestigious and noble range that can be brought into the dining room, ideal for serving and flambéing.
Care of copper: use a polishing paste.
Care of stainless steel: wash by hand.


	2 mm thick copper
	Handle: Brass
	For all cooktops EXCEPT induction.


Beware of scams: some utensils presented as stainless steel copper have a thin veneer of copper and none of its culinary qualities. The copper-stainless steel INOCUIVRE DE BUYER is an assurance of quality cooking with a material composed of 90% copper and 10% stainless steel, a single-piece skirt and bottom. This material diffuses heat evenly and quickly: not only on the bottom, but also on the sides; a heating circuit is created and the food is cooked evenly. Ideal for delicate cooking, for sauces and generally for reduction cooking. Riveted brass long handle and side handles.

Copper:


	Best heat conductor: uniform distribution and ideal transmission of heat.
	Allows greater control of the cooking environment.
	Preserves the natural colour of fruit: jams do not brown.
	Large flat bottom: very stable pan even on a gas burner.
	Rounded angle formed by the skirt and flat bottom: allows you to work all the mixture; easy maintenance.
	Brass handles: easy handling; safe use.
	Open rolled edge: hygienic; no protruding edge.
	Care: soak. Easy cleaning with a special copper polish paste.
	Dishwasher-safe but without alkaline detergent cleaning.
	Suitable for all cooktops EXCEPT induction.


Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

item_description: de Buyer - B Bois - Mineral B Element Frypan - High temperature: Perfect for sealing, browning and grilling
overview:
The ‘B BOIS’ line is the result of the meeting of 2 worlds, 2 brands and offers a whole range of new products, from frying pan to spice mills, including essential utensils. The beech wood used is a PEFC certified wood coming from sustainably managed forests. It is natural and protected with beeswax. The ‘B BOIS’ products are exclusively sold through physical retailers.

The essential MINERAL B ELEMENT steel range comes with a beech wood handle protected by beeswax, as for the pan body. Always essential for high temperature cooking, for sealing, frying and browning.

The MINERAL B ELEMENT frying pan has a long iron handle firmly fastened with rivets. It features a true Lyonnaise shape: the side is high, flared and rounded, without any sharp angles, which means it lets the food slide easily from the pan to the dish or plate. The fry pan becomes naturally non-stick the more it is used thanks to the seasoning process. The extra-strong iron of this pan is suitable for intense sources of heat, including induction. The MINERAL B ELEMENT pan will be part of your kitchenware for decades, as long as you follow the specific instructions to use and clean the iron utensils.

Iron and cast iron pans are not suitable for scrambled eggs. Please only prepare setting eggs and omelets in a well-baked pan.

ORGANIC BEE WAX FINISH


	Bees produce by-products that are well known in nature. de Buyer discovered that bee wax is an excellent option to protect their pans against oxidation.
	Bee wax helps in the seasoning process.
	Bee wax improves the nonstick quality.


IRON=100 % NATURAL MINERAL MATERIAL


	Guaranteed without PTFE and PFOA. No chemicals added.
	Environment safe production.
	Ecological, recyclable, and good for the environment.
                
                Can be used on all types of cooker - incl. induction
                All information about the pans from de Buyer you can find on data sheets tab.


COOKING QUALITY


	High temperature: Perfect for SEALING, BROWNING and GRILLING
	Improved flavour.


TRADITIONAL


	Authentic design: «Lyonnaise» shape. Curved handle «à la Française». Symbol of French Gastronomy.


SEASONING


	The more the pan is used, the better the performance.
	The darker it becomes the better it is for natural nonstick properties.


FOR ALL HOTPLATES INCLUDING INDUCTION.

The manufacturer does not guarantee the flatness of the floor, if the diameter of the heat source deviates more than 1,5cm in relation to the bottom of the pan. There are no restrictions for the use of iron pans on full surface induction and gas stoves.

item_description: de Buyer - Copper Conical Jam Pan 12 L - Traditional jam pan in copper with riveted cast-iron handles. Pan ideal for making jams.
overview:
Traditional jam pan in copper with riveted cast-iron handles. Pan ideal for making jams.
Made with solid copper, this traditional jam pan has exceptional thermal properties: it offers quick and uniform heat diffusion, maintaining the natural colour of the fruit without browning it. It can be used to boil sugar and/or fruit to make jellies, jams or preserves. Fitted with 2 side handles, riveted on, it is easy and safe to handle. Resistant and durable, the copper jam pan is designed for intense and professional use. Its large flat base provides excellent stability during use. The open rolled rim makes the pan easy to clean and does not cause injuries. For any heat source EXCEPT induction.


	100 % COPPER
	2 mm thick copper
	Ø= 40 cm x H= 14,5 cm - V= 12 L
	Weight: 3.4kg
	Handle: iron cast
	For all cooktops EXCEPT induction.

	Best heat conductor: uniform distribution and ideal transmission of heat.
	Allows greater control of the cooking environment.
	Preserves the natural colour of fruit: jams do not brown.
	Large flat bottom: very stable pan even on a gas burner.
	Rounded angle formed by the skirt and flat bottom: allows you to work all the mixture; easy maintenance.
	Cast-iron handles: easy handling; safe use.
	Open rolled edge: hygienic; no protruding edge.
	Care: soak. Easy cleaning with a special copper polish paste.
	Dishwasher-safe but without alkaline detergent cleaning.
	Suitable for all cooktops EXCEPT induction.


Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

	Several shapes and sizes available




The Mango Passion Fruit Vanilla Yule Log
by Jonathan Mougel

For a log mould 30 cm ref 3206.30 + mini log mould ref 3206.04

Ingredients

Almond genoise


	2 whole eggs
	65 g of sugar
	65 g of T55 flour
	25 g of ground almonds


Crisp


	68 g of almond praline
	10 g of white chocolate
	1/4 vanilla pod
	40 g of crumbled gavotte biscuits
	1 g of Fleur de sel
	20 g Toasted chopped almonds


Mango-passion fruit filling


	75 g of Mango Pulp
	200 g of Pineapple brunoise
	 2 g of Ginger
	26 g of Brown sugar
	Lime zest of one lime
	1,2 g of Agar-agar
	1.5 g of NH Pectin (or 1.5 g of gelatin rehydrated in cold water)
	2 g of gelatin


Vanilla Mousse


	170 g of milk
	2 g of vanilla
	50 g of egg yolks
	30 g of sugar
	3 g of gelatin
	100 g of white chocolate
	240 g of whipped cream


Exotic glaze


	250 g of exotic fruit pulp
	50 gof lemon pulp
	75 g of glucose
	100 g of sugar
	5 g of NH pectin (or 4 g of gelatin rehydrated in cold water)
	5 g of gelatin
	100 g of neutral mirror glaze



Instructions

Almond genoise (for 1 sheet of 40x30cm) (1 base 7x29.5cm) :


	Whisk the eggs and sugar in a double boiler until the mixture is frothy. Do not exceed 50°C
	Add  the sifted flour. Then add the almonds gently.
	Spread on a 40x30 sheet.
	Bake for 8 minutes at 175°C.
	Cut a base of 7x29.5cm.


Crisp (138 g per log) :

Melt the white chocolate and add the vanilla.


	Then add the praline and mix.
	Mix all the ingredients together.
	Spread over the base.
	Set aside.


Mango-passion fruit filling :


	Cook all the ingredients for 2 min.
	Add the gelatin with the lime zest at the end.
	Pour into a mini log mould and freeze the filling.


Vanilla mousse (590 g per mould) :


	Heat the milk, vanilla, egg yolks and sugar.
	Cook everything at 85°C.
	Add the melted gelatin and pour it all into the chocolate.
	Mix. Add the whipped cream when it reaches 23°C.


Exotic glaze :


	To be prepared the day before.
	Heat the pulps to 40°C, add the pectin-sugar mixture.
	Bring to the boil.
	Add the previously hydrated gelatin, then the mirror glaze..
	Mix.
	The next day, heat to 30°C and mix.
	Glaze the log.


Assembly and finishing :


	Place an acetate sheet in the bottom of the mould.
	Pour in the vanilla mousse and immerse the exotic filling..
	Seal with the base/crisp.
	Freeze for at least 6 hours.
	Unmould and glaze with the exotic glaze.
	Decorate.

It can be used to prepare large amounts of food and its high sides limit splashes when cooking meat. It becomes naturally non-stick the more it is used thanks to the seasoning process. The extra-strong iron of this pan is suitable for intense sources of heat, including induction. The MINERAL B ELEMENT pan will be part of your kitchenware for decades, as long as you follow the specific instructions to use and clean the iron utensils.

Iron and cast iron pans are not suitable for scrambled eggs. Please only prepare setting eggs and omelets in a well-baked pan.

De Buyer Removables: in 3 ranges, 3 materials, 3 uses with a single, practical and ergonomic long handle with a velvety feel. The removable iron version of Sealing, Browning and Grilling.

TWISTY removable long handle

Tomato jelly

	50 g of chicken broth
	2 g of gelatine
	25 g of chopped Kalamata olives
	25 g of chopped sundried tomatoes



Instructions

Boil the tomato juice and add the gelatine that you have previously rehydrated in cold water. Mix well and pour into the bottom of the mould.
Leave to set in the fridge.


	Boil the chicken stock (white stock) and the cream.
	Melt the gelatine that has previously been rehydrated in cold water.
	Pour in 3 batches over the fresh goat's cheese, and whisk to obtain a smooth mixture.
	Add the olives and tomatoes.


Mix and pour over the tomato jelly.
Leave to set in the fridge for at least 3 hours.

Enjoy with rocket salad for example.

overview:

This wok is made with stainless steel copper specially designed for induction. It heats up quickly and provides a perfect heating circuit across the entire vessel for even cooking. The copper wok has a 10 cm ø base that makes it perfectly stable on any cook top. It is fitted with two side handles, which only take up a little space. Its generous 4-litre volume makes it perfect to prepare many family meals.

WORLDWIDE UNIQUENESS COPPER COOKWARE NOW INDUCTION READY


	Ø 32 cm  (Ø Bottom = 10 cm) H= 12 cm  V= 4 Liter
	90% COPPER- 10% STAINLESS STEEL
	2-mm thick copper stainless steel - Gloss polished
	Special innovative ferro-magnetic bottom
	for all hotplates including INDUCTION
	Riveted ergonomic handles made of cast st/steel.
	Special innovative ferro-magnetic bottom
	Excellent conductor of heat
	Uniform cooking through both bottom and sides
	Ideal for reducing and simmering


PRIMA MATERA - For cooking and for presentation on table. Copper is an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. This material ensures quick and homogeneous cooking in the whole pan: not only in the bottom but also on the side to obtain a perfect heat distribution which makes copper pans ideal for delicate preparations, such as sauces.
 




Outer COPPER - the colour changes on a heat sources. Use a special copper cleaner to give brilliance back to a copper utensil. Never use a metal sponge. The stainless steel inner wall allows for easy care. There is no need for replating as for the traditional tin-plated copper.

Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Utensils 2 mm thick are intended for professional use. Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

Handles in cast steel, curved, fixed by strong rivets. Copper-stainless steel: this is a bimetal laminate.

The outer sides are made of very thick copper and allow excellent heat conduction. The inner side, made of stainless steel, allow for easy care of the pans and there is no need for replating. Polished outer finish. Mountings in cast steel, stainless steel, or bronze, fixed by strong rivets.

It can be used to boil sugar and/or fruit to make jellies, jams or preserves. Fitted with 2 side handles, riveted on, it is easy and safe to handle. Resistant and durable, the copper jam pan is designed for intense and professional use. Its large flat base provides excellent stability during use. The open rolled rim makes the pan easy to clean and does not cause injuries.

	1,2 mm thick copper - 1,5 Kg
	Ø= 38 cm x H= 15 cm - V= 9 L

	Best heat conductor: uniform distribution and ideal transmission of heat.

	Preserves the natural colour of fruit: jams do not brown.

	Dishwasher-safe but without alkaline detergent cleaning.


Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

item_description: de Buyer - Non-stick Ceramic Crepe pan - CERANOA - The crêpe pan is designed to provide even, controlled cooking - essential for making perfect crêpes.
overview:
Not available until mid-September.
CERANOA - The new PFAS-free non-stick reference.

The CERANOA crêpe pan is designed to provide even, controlled cooking - essential for making perfect crêpes. Its forged aluminum body ensures quick and uniform heat-up and cool-down, allowing for precise cooking every time.
The thick forged aluminum construction ensures excellent resistance to warping and impact for your CERANOA non-stick frying pan.

Compatible with all stovetops, including induction, your ceramic frying pan adapts to any kitchen. To preserve it for a long time, it’s recommended to gradually increase the heat when using this non-stick pan.

Aluminum is a great heat conductor - it heats up quickly and distributes heat evenly. Non-stick coatings are sensitive to overheating and thermal shock and are better suited for gentle cooking. When using it on induction, there is no need to use the booster function, and we recommend not using the cooktop at full power.

Versatile and convenient, the de Buyer ceramic frying pan can also be used in the oven, provided the temperature does not exceed 200°C (392°F) for a maximum of 10 minutes—this is known as “flash oven” use. This PFAS-free pan is made from recycled aluminum.

Use a lid and let your saucy dishes simmer in peace with the CERANOA ceramic frying pan. Although the ceramic coating is abrasion-resistant, we do not recommend using metal utensils in your CERANOA non-stick pan.


	Ø 26 cm - Ø Bottom = 22,6 cm - Length with handle: 50 cm - H = 2 cm - W = 0,9 kg
	Ø 32 cm - Ø Bottom = 26,8 cm - Length with handle: 50 cm - H = 2 cm - W = 1,1 kg


Benefits of the CERANOA Frying Pan:


	Long-lasting durability
	Exceptional glide and easy to clean
	PFAS-free
	Made from recycled aluminum
	Made in Europe
	Body material: recycled forged aluminum
	Interior coating: smooth ceramic
	Exterior coating: elegant matte black
	Lyonnaise shape: ideal for flipping food while cooking
	Riveted strip handle, French-style curved
	Non-stick pan compatible with all stovetops including induction
	Oven flash use: up to 200°C for max. 10 minutes
	Uses: sautéing, simmering, and reheating
	Care: to extend the life of your ceramic coating, dishwasher use is not recommended. Prefer handwashing with warm soapy water and a non-abrasive sponge.

item_description: de Buyer - Non-stick Ceramic Curved Sauté-pan - CERANOA -  The new PFAS-free non-stick reference.
overview:
CERANOA - The new PFAS-free non-stick reference.

The forged aluminum frying pan with its PFAS-free ceramic coating is perfect for preparing delicate dishes (fish, vegetables, sauces, etc.). This 24 cm Ceramic Frying Pan is designed for use at medium to low temperatures. It is therefore ideal for simmering, reducing, reheating, or sautéing.
The thick forged aluminum construction ensures excellent resistance to warping and impact for your CERANOA non-stick frying pan.

Compatible with all stovetops, including induction, your ceramic frying pan adapts to any kitchen. To preserve it for a long time, it’s recommended to gradually increase the heat when using this non-stick pan.

Aluminum is a great heat conductor - it heats up quickly and distributes heat evenly. Non-stick coatings are sensitive to overheating and thermal shock and are better suited for gentle cooking. When using it on induction, there is no need to use the booster function, and we recommend not using the cooktop at full power.

The CERANOA curved sauté pan is the perfect ally for healthy, precise cooking. Its PFAS-free ceramic non-stick coating offers outstanding glide. Contrary to popular belief, a non-stick sauté pan is a true expert at sautéing, simmering, and reheating your dishes. Ideal for low to medium heat cooking, it preserves the flavor and texture of ingredients while minimizing the need for added fats.

Use a lid and let your saucy dishes simmer in peace with the CERANOA ceramic frying pan. Although the ceramic coating is abrasion-resistant, we do not recommend using metal utensils in your CERANOA non-stick pan.


	
	Ø 24 cm - Ø Bottom = 18 cm - Length with handle: 43 cm - H = 4,5 cm - W = 0,96 kg
	Ø 28 cm - Ø Bottom = 22 cm - Length with handle: 50 cm - H = 4,5 cm - W = 1,28 kg
	


Benefits of the CERANOA Frying Pan:


	Long-lasting durability
	Exceptional glide and easy to clean
	PFAS-free
	Made from recycled aluminum
	Made in Europe
	Body material: recycled forged aluminum
	Interior coating: smooth ceramic
	Exterior coating: elegant matte black
	Lyonnaise shape: ideal for flipping food while cooking
	Riveted strip handle, French-style curved
	Non-stick pan compatible with all stovetops including induction
	Oven flash use: up to 200°C for max. 10 minutes
	Uses: sautéing, simmering, and reheating
	Care: to extend the life of your ceramic coating, dishwasher use is not recommended. Prefer handwashing with warm soapy water and a non-abrasive sponge.

item_description: de Buyer - Mineral B Element Pro - Omelette Pan - 28 cm - Have a specific rounded side, designed especially for flipping omelettes
overview:
Perfect for delicious omelets: With the rounded sides, omelets can be turned perfectly and become crispy on the outside and soft on the inside thanks to the perfect heat distribution.

de Buyer - Round iron pan with cast stainless steel handle - Mineral B Element Pro - For browning and grilling food, the de Buyer iron pan is a must. Once the iron skillet reaches temperatures above 140°C, it seals the food to the surface as the food's natural juices caramelize (the Maillard reaction). The crust that forms keeps moisture from evaporating. As a result, the food becomes brown and crispy on the surface and remains tender and juicy in the center. Perfect for frying meat and potatoes and for preparing crispy crepes and delicious omelettes!

Iron and cast iron pans are not suitable for scrambled eggs. Please prepare set eggs and omletts only in a well-baked pan.


	Note that the de Buyer iron pan is baked before first use (see below: Baking, Questions and Tips).


A pan made of sheet steel must be baked in. Over the course of use, it will turn black. The more a pan has been used, the better it cooks; the blacker it is, the less it sticks. Dishwasher and cleaning products must not be used.

overview:
The de Buyer sauté pan is ideal for sautéing vegetables or pieces of meat. It is used to brown foods quickly. Its low edge avoids condensation on the sides and lets humidity evaporate, thereby improving caramelisation of the juices. Its shape lets you handle and flip food without having it fall out. It is suitable for any heating source, except induction, and is also oven-safe. The long, elegant, ergonomic cast stainless steel handle, firmly fastened with rivets, guarantees a natural and comfortable grip. The stainless steel interior is practical, hygienic and easy to clean.


	Ø 24 cm  (Ø Bottom = 16,3 cm)  H= 8,2 cm - V= 3,1 L
	90% COPPER- 10% STAINLESS STEEL
	2-mm thick copper stainless steel - Gloss polished
	for all hotplates - except induction
	Riveted ergonomic handles made of cast st/steel.
	Excellent conductor of heat
	Uniform cooking through both bottom and sides
	Ideal for reducing and simmering


Inocuivre - For cooking and for presentation on table. Copper is an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. This material ensures quick and homogeneous cooking in the whole pan: not only in the bottom but also on the side to obtain a perfect heat distribution which makes copper pans ideal for delicate preparations, such as sauces.









Outer COPPER - the colour changes on a heat sources. Use a special copper cleaner to give brilliance back to a copper utensil. Never use a metal sponge. The stainless steel inner wall allows for easy care. There is no need for replating as for the traditional tin-plated copper.



Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Utensils 2 mm thick are intended for professional use. Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

Handles in cast steel, curved, fixed by strong rivets. Copper-stainless steel: this is a bimetal laminate.

The outer sides are made of very thick copper and allow excellent heat conduction. The inner side, made of stainless steel, allow for easy care of the pans and there is no need for replating. Polished outer finish. Mountings in cast steel, stainless steel, or bronze, fixed by strong rivets.

The rounded sauté pan is ideal for reduction cooking as its small base heats food quickly and its wide top diameter ensures greater evaporation while stirring the food. It is suitable for any heating source, including induction, and is also oven-safe. The long, elegant, ergonomic cast stainless steel handle, firmly fastened with rivets, guarantees a natural and comfortable grip. The stainless steel interior is practical, hygienic and easy to clean.

WORLDWIDE UNIQUENESS COPPER COOKWARE NOW INDUCTION READY


	Ø 24 cm  (Ø Bottom = 16,3 cm)  H= 8,2 cm - V= 3,1 L
	90% COPPER- 10% STAINLESS STEEL

	Special innovative ferro-magnetic bottom
	for all hotplates including INDUCTION
	Riveted ergonomic handles made of cast st/steel.
	Special innovative ferro-magnetic bottom
	Excellent conductor of heat
	Uniform cooking through both bottom and sides
	Ideal for reducing and simmering


PRIMA MATERA - For cooking and for presentation on table. Copper is an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. This material ensures quick and homogeneous cooking in the whole pan: not only in the bottom but also on the side to obtain a perfect heat distribution which makes copper pans ideal for delicate preparations, such as sauces.



The manufacturer does not guarantee the flatness of the floor, if the diameter of the heat source deviates more than 1,5cm in relation to the bottom of the pan. There are no restrictions for the use of iron pans on full surface induction and gas stoves.
 




Outer COPPER - the colour changes on a heat sources. Use a special copper cleaner to give brilliance back to a copper utensil. Never use a metal sponge. The stainless steel inner wall allows for easy care. There is no need for replating as for the traditional tin-plated copper.

Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Utensils 2 mm thick are intended for professional use. Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

Handles in cast steel, curved, fixed by strong rivets. Copper-stainless steel: this is a bimetal laminate.

The outer sides are made of very thick copper and allow excellent heat conduction. The inner side, made of stainless steel, allow for easy care of the pans and there is no need for replating. Polished outer finish. Mountings in cast steel, stainless steel, or bronze, fixed by strong rivets.

item_description: de Buyer - Round Grill Frypan - Mineral B Element - High temperature: Perfect for sealing, browning and grilling
overview:
Juicy and tender, with distinctive grill marks on each side—that’s how a steak should be. Properly searing steaks, chicken, or turkey filets is simply perfect with the iron grill pan. The grill ridges effectively retain fat, allowing the food to keep its pure, natural flavor.

For browning and grilling food, the de Buyer iron pan is a must. Once the iron pan reaches temperatures above 140 °C, it seals the surface of the food, as the natural juices caramelize (the Maillard reaction). The resulting crust prevents moisture from evaporating. The food becomes brown and crispy on the outside while remaining tender and juicy inside. Perfect for searing meat and potatoes, and preparing crispy crêpes and delicious omelets!

Iron and cast iron pans are not suitable for scrambled eggs. Prepare fried eggs and omelets only in a well-seasoned pan.


	Please note that the de Buyer iron pan must be seasoned before first use (see below: Seasoning, FAQs, Tips)


A sheet steel pan must be seasoned. Over time, it turns black. The more a pan is used, the better it performs; the blacker it is, the less it sticks. Dishwashers and cleaning agents must not be used.

item_description: de Buyer Marlux - Mill Rumba 20 cm - Small handy mill with a patented continuously variable grinding.
overview:
de Buyer Marlux - Rumba Pepper mill / salt mill / Spice mill - 20 cm - Small handy mill with a patented continuously variable quality grinding of carbon steel (pepper) and 18/10 stainless (salt)


	H = 20 cm - Ø 6,5 cm
	Grinder pepper mills: infinitely adjustable grinder made of carbon steel. Two different tooth rings lead the peppercorns and keep these in breaking. They are different shaped and cured, thus they are sharp and are virtually indestructible.
	Grinder salt mills: continuously variable conical grinder made of 18/10 stainless steel. A special finish protects the steel particularly resistant to salt corrosion. The milled salt grains have all the same size. The grinder is designed for grinding large, dry salt crystals. The inner shaft is made of stainless steel.
	Grinder spice mills: Ceramic mechanism. A ceramic grinder is broadly used for any spice in the kitchen or on the table: all the peppers corns, white and sea salt, black salt from Hawaï, pink salt from Himalaya, dry herbs, caraway, poppy or sesame seeds …
	Material: Made of beechwood, beeswax finishing
	Marlux mechanism
	Made in France




From a factory that began making mills in 1875, Marlux was created in 1938 and is now renowned for its 3rd-generation family expertise. With its long history in manufacturing, Marlux is a leader in the pepper and salt mill industry in France and worldwide.
Marlux is well-known for the precision of its salt and pepper grinders manufactured on the company’s proprietary machinery, for its original designs, and for the reliability of its production.
Thanks to the exceptional quality of the products and the efficiency of the team, Marlux has built a successful export business throughout Europe and across several continents.

Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Utensils 2 mm thick are intended for professional use. Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

item_description: de Buyer Marlux - Mill Rumba B Bois - Small handy mill with a patented continuously variable grinding.
overview:
de Buyer - Pepper and Salt Mill - 10 cm - Small handy mill with a patented continuously variable quality grinding of carbon steel (pepper) and 18/10 stainless (salt)


	Grinder pepper mills: infinitely adjustable grinder made of carbon steel. Two different tooth rings lead the peppercorns and keep these in breaking. They are different shaped and cured, thus they are sharp and are virtually indestructible.
	Grinder salt mills: continuously variable conical grinder made of 18/10 stainless steel. A special finish protects the steel particularly resistant to salt corrosion. The milled salt grains have all the same size. The grinder is designed for grinding large, dry salt crystals. The inner shaft is made of stainless steel.
	Grinder spice mills: Ceramic mechanism. A ceramic grinder is broadly used for any spice in the kitchen or on the table: all the peppers corns, white and sea salt, black salt from Hawaï, pink salt from Himalaya, dry herbs, caraway, poppy or sesame seeds …
	Material: Made of beechwood, beeswax finishing
	
	Made in France

PRIMA MATERA - For cooking and for presentation on table. Copper is an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. Outer COPPER - the colour changes on a heat sources. Use a special copper cleaner to give brilliance back to a copper utensil. Never use a metal sponge. The stainless steel inner wall allows for easy care. There is no need for replating as for the traditional tin-plated copper.



Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

item_description: de Buyer - Copper Sauté Pan - Prima Matera - Inocuivre Induction - is ideal for sautéing vegetables or pieces of meat
overview:
The de Buyer sauté pan is ideal for sautéing vegetables or pieces of meat. It is used to brown foods quickly. Its low edge avoids condensation on the sides and lets humidity evaporate, thereby improving caramelisation of the juices. Its shape lets you handle and flip food without having it fall out. It is suitable for any heating source, including induction, and is also oven-safe. The long, elegant, ergonomic cast stainless steel handle, firmly fastened with rivets, guarantees a natural and comfortable grip. The stainless steel interior is practical, hygienic and easy to clean.

The utensils in the de Buyer PRIMA MATERA range are 90% copper and 10% stainless steel on the inside. Copper is the best heat conductor and ensures a perfect heating circuit throughout the entire container. It provides exceptional cooking performance on any cook top, including induction, through the special iron-magnetic stainless steel INDUCTION bottom.
Elegant and fluid, the ergonomic long handles and side handles of the range are made with cast stainless steel.

WORLDWIDE UNIQUENESS COPPER COOKWARE NOW INDUCTION READY


	Ø 20 cm  (Ø Bottom = 17,5 cm)  H= 5,8 cm - V= 1,8 L
	90% COPPER- 10% STAINLESS STEEL
	2-mm thick copper stainless steel - Gloss polished
	Special innovative ferro-magnetic bottom
	for all hotplates including INDUCTION
	Riveted ergonomic handles made of cast st/steel.
	Copper Stewpan with magnetic bottom INDUCTION and st/steel lid
	Excellent conductor of heat
	Uniform cooking through both bottom and sides
	Ideal for reducing and simmering


PRIMA MATERA - For cooking and for presentation on table. Copper is an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. This material ensures quick and homogeneous cooking in the whole pan: not only in the bottom but also on the side to obtain a perfect heat distribution which makes copper pans ideal for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

The manufacturer does not guarantee the flatness of the floor, if the diameter of the heat source deviates more than 1,5cm in relation to the bottom of the pan. There are no restrictions for the use of iron pans on full surface induction and gas stoves.
 




Outer COPPER - the colour changes on a heat sources. Use a special copper cleaner to give brilliance back to a copper utensil. Never use a metal sponge. The stainless steel inner wall allows for easy care. There is no need for replating as for the traditional tin-plated copper.

Authenticity

The nobility of copper in the kitchen arts is not only die to its warm and colourful looks, but also and essentially to its capacity to conduct heat, which makes copper pans idel for delicate preparations, such as sauces.

Care

To clean copper, use a special easy-to-use-paste, preferable after each use. For the inner stainless steel or tin-plated surfaces, wash in the ordinary way. Cleaning in the dishwasher is not recommended because dishwasher liquids contain high levels of chlorine.

Technical features

Copper is an exeptional metal, an extraordinary conductor of heat, particularly suitable for French cooking and reducing. However, copper should not be in direct contact with most foods: as a result it is used in combination with another metal (for the inside of the pans).

Tin-plated copper: The traditional tin-plating process guarantees a thick enough coating of tin for professional use. The lenght of time the pan can be used before being replated is therefor longer. But beware: do not heat to over 260°C.

Utensils 2 mm thick are intended for professional use. Polished outer finish, smooth or hammered effect.

Handles in cast steel, curved, fixed by strong rivets. Copper-stainless steel: this is a bimetal laminate.

The outer sides are made of very thick copper and allow excellent heat conduction. The inner side, made of stainless steel, allow for easy care of the pans and there is no need for replating. Polished outer finish. Mountings in cast steel, stainless steel, or bronze, fixed by strong rivets.
